pi_items.metadata_value: Simulated Upper Mississippi River Floodplain Surveys
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetically generated generic wetland data via Python script

pi_items.source: Empirical calculation of Shannon diversity (base e) on the generated CSV data
Empirical calculation of Shannon diversity (base e) on the generated CSV data
Empirical calculation of Simpson diversity (1-D) on the generated CSV data
Empirical calculation of Simpson diversity (1-D) on the generated CSV data

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetically generated dataset of wetland bird surveys covering the years 2019-2023. Site WM-03 is explicitly generated as the pristine site, while WM-09 is generated as the degraded site. The data encodes declining population trends for the Rusty Blackbird, Black Tern, and American Bittern, and increasing trends for the Canada Goose and Sandhill Crane. Empirical calculation on the generated data yields a Shannon diversity index (base e) ranging from 0.78 to 2.909, and a Simpson diversity index (1-D) ranging from 0.439 to 0.938, contradicting the heuristic ranges claimed in the metadata.
